title: Debugbar trigger Livewire snapshot verfication
author: ghabriel25

title: Inertia tab truncates page parameter
author: dj-sg
body:
Version in use : 4.3.0

It seems the output of page parameters on the Inertia tab in debugbar are truncated for nested arrays. 
For example the content of an loaded relation items is replaced with _cut: <count of items>.

I could not find a setting to control this kind of behavior. 

Any guidance how to increase output limitations would be appreciated.

title: blade views path
author: ed-vieira
body: Is there any way to enable the old feature of displaying view paths? I can't find anything about it. The new "click to open" behavior doesn't work well in several scenarios. In code editors like VS Code, the file opens in a new window separate from the project. In Antigravity, the window opens Antigravity 2.0 but doesn't open the Antigravity IDE. In Windows WSL, although complicated, it's possible to configure it to have the same behavior of opening in a new window unrelated to the already open project, which isn't ideal. I just wanted the old behavior back: copying the view path from the debug bar and pasting it into the VS Code search. It was simple and very helpful. I understand the display space issue, but it could be displayed as a simple button to click and copy instead of the entire text, or at least the path could be displayed below as an alternative to be copied.

title: Link to Source - Livewire MFC
author: schoenbergerb
body: If you use Livewire’s [Multi-file components](https://livewire.laravel.com/docs/4.x/components#multi-file-components)￼, the “Go to source” link currently points to the compiled component instead of the original source file. It would be much more helpful if the link resolved to the actual source file so it can be edited directly.

title: start_measure(), stop_measure(), measure() are removed since v4 but not removed from readme
author: theticraft
body:
I tried to use start_measure() and stop_measure() as I used to. After a little bit of research, I found out that the helpers were removed in v4. Instead, you should use debugbar()->start_measure() and debugbar()->stop_measure().
I found it confusing because I used it in the same way as it was documented. But I didn't get any warning about this being deprecated. Instead, it was a fatal PHP error.
Could you bring them back or at least change the readme?

title: Livewire `RequireLivewireHeaders` middleware appended twice after component action
author: ghabriel25
body:
### Laravel 13 & Livewre 4
 
When inspecting middleware from Livewire component. The **`RequireLivewireHeaders`** middleware appended twice after component action. See (https://github.com/livewire/livewire/pull/10288) eventhough this middleware only called once per livewire request (Confirmed using logger inside this middleware)

This is really crucial because I just open PR there and come with solution that solve this. However that was not the case and after some debug using logger, I found that this middleware not triggered twice but only once.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/31fe7eb8-70a8-4c8b-9470-11bba270ff43

title: Feature request - Copy collector / rows as markdown
author: barryvdh
body:
Use-case: provide context to AI

Idea: Add some context to different tabs, or specific row. For example 'controller' can use a button to give the primary content as markdown to an AI. Or a query with bindings and location.


Possible methods
 - Add a 'summary' method to a collector (eg. Summarized interface), which has a button on the tab
 - Add a summary field for entries (like queries)
 - Let the Debugbar/UI figure out how to copy relevant stuff (how?)

title: Validation errors are not shown
author: miken32
body:
I am unable to view validation error messages in the Session panel. When I try to view the `errors` object, the output is truncated like [this](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fc63e47a-56bd-4921-ab54-8a8557f6543b):

```none
Illuminate\Support\ViewErrorBag {#2065 ▼
    #bags: array:1 [▼
        "default" => Illuminate\Support\MessageBag {#1512 ▼
            #messages: array:2 [▼

                …2
            ]
            #format: ":message"
        }
    ]
}
```

I've checked config and I don't see any settings that would apply here. Any suggestions?

(I think a dedicated validation collector might be useful, to show what rules were applied to the request, which ones failed, etc.)

title: PhpDebugBar is not defined with FormRequest::failOnUnknownFields - livewire template
author: xorock
body: Hi. I've set up a new project from a Livewire template. I added the line FormRequest::failOnUnknownFields() to the AppServiceProvider. It turns out that after refreshing the templates, it's impossible to load the Debugbar. The environment is on Apache, I have a custom domain configured, and it seems that instead of loading the assets, it's loading the main page. This causes a 404 error and ultimately an Uncaught ReferenceError: PhpDebugBar is not defined. After commenting out the FormRequest::failOnUnknownFields() line, everything returns to normal. Is there a way to work around this (except of wrapping code with if (app()->isProduction())?

title: \DebugBar\DataFormatter\QueryFormatter::quoteBinding() unable to handle a resource as binding
author: aedart
body:
In situations, when you dealing with resource as binding, the `quoteBinding()` will fail because it always expects a string as `$binding`.
In my case, I store small files in the database, meaning that bindings contain resources, rather than strings.

Version: `v4.2.6`

### Possible Solution

The `quoteBinding()` could perhaps be changed to accept and deal with resources. However, I'm not sure if the entire chain before invoking `quoteBinding()` should be refactored to deal with resources accordingly.

### Similar issue in Laravel

I once encountered a similar issue in Laravel. Back then, I made the following pull request was made and merged into the framework: https://github.com/laravel/framework/pull/53100

Sadly, I'm too unfamiliar with "DebugBar" code to be able to offer the same (_at least for the moment_).

### Work-around

Thus far, the only way to avoid this is to disable debugbar (_as far as I can see_).

title: [FEAT] Checkbox toggle for Model::shouldBeStrict
author: xtay2
body:
### Background
I'm in the middle of migrating my legacy codebase to use the `Model::shouldBeStrict(!app()->isProduction())` setting in the `AppServiceProvider::boot` method. Because this can't be done in a single commit / merge request, the setting is now set on the main branch, but interferes with all other branches for which I have to turn it off. It would be great to be able to toggle this setting on or off from the ui.

### Feature description
Introduce a new tab in the debug bar that contains multiple checkboxes for all common development settings:
- Model::preventLazyLoading
- Model::preventAccessingMissingAttributes
- Model::preventSilentlyDiscardingAttributes

title: Laravel Debugbar toolbar only loads on first request when using Octane + FrankenPHP
author: faraweilyas
body:
I'm experiencing an issue where the **Laravel Debugbar toolbar only appears on the first request**, but does not appear on subsequent requests unless Octane reloads.

This happens consistently when running **Laravel Octane with FrankenPHP**.

After the initial request loads the Debugbar correctly, further page requests do not show the toolbar anymore. However, if a file change occurs and **Octane reloads (via `--watch`)**, the Debugbar appears again for the next request, but then disappears again for subsequent ones.

### Environment

**Framework & Packages**

```
"laravel/framework": "^12.0",
"laravel/octane": "^2.13",
"fruitcake/laravel-debugbar": "^4.1",
"livewire/livewire": "^4.0",
"livewire/blaze": "^1.0"
```

**PHP**

```
PHP 8.5
```

**Server**

```
Laravel Octane + FrankenPHP
```

**Frontend**

```
Livewire v4 (components are lazy-loaded)
```

```php
<livewire:product.some-component-a lazy="on-load" />

<livewire:product.some-component-b lazy />

<livewire:product.some-component-c lazy />
```

### Attempted Fix

I tried disabling the following config option to see if it affected the behaviour, but it made no difference:

```php
'ajax_handler_auto_show' => env('DEBUGBAR_AJAX_HANDLER_AUTO_SHOW', false),
```

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install Laravel Debugbar in a Laravel 12 project
2. Run the application using Octane with FrankenPHP

```
php artisan octane:start --watch --server=frankenphp
```

3. Load the application in the browser
4. Debugbar appears on the first request
5. Navigate or reload the page
6. Debugbar no longer appears
7. Make a code change so Octane reloads
8. Debugbar appears again for the first request after reload

### Expected Behaviour

The Debugbar toolbar should appear on every request when `APP_DEBUG=true`.

### Actual Behaviour

Debugbar appears only on the **first request after an Octane reload**, and disappears for subsequent requests.

### Notes

Since Octane keeps the application in memory between requests, this may be related to **state persistence between requests**, but I'm unsure where the issue originates (Debugbar vs Octane vs FrankenPHP vs Livewire).

Do you have any idea? Thank you for your support.

title: MailCollectorProvider timeline throws exception due to spl_object_id mismatch between MessageSending and MessageSent
author: bretterer
body:
## Description

The `MailCollectorProvider` uses `spl_object_id($e->message)` to correlate `MessageSending` and `MessageSent` events for the timeline. However, the `->message` property returns **different object instances** in each event, causing `stopMeasure()` to throw a `DebugBarException` because the measure key never matches.

## Root Cause

In `src/CollectorProviders/MailCollectorProvider.php` (lines 30-31):

```php
$events->listen(MessageSending::class, fn(MessageSending $e) => $timeCollector->startMeasure('mail' . spl_object_id($e->message), 'Mail: ' . $e->message->getSubject()));
$events->listen(MessageSent::class, fn(MessageSent $e) => $timeCollector->stopMeasure('mail' . spl_object_id($e->message)));
```

- `MessageSending->message` is a direct public property — the `Symfony\Component\Mime\Email` instance.
- `MessageSent->message` is accessed via `__get()` magic, which calls `$this->sent->getOriginalMessage()`. This returns a **different object instance** than the one from `MessageSending`.

Because `spl_object_id()` returns a different ID for each object instance, `startMeasure('mail2415')` is called on `MessageSending`, but `stopMeasure('mail2416')` is called on `MessageSent`. The `stopMeasure` call then throws:

```
Failed stopping measure 'mail2416' because it hasn't been started
```

## Impact

This exception is uncaught and bubbles up into application code. In our case, order creation wraps mail-sending listeners in a try/catch, and this Debugbar exception causes the entire operation to fail in local development.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Send any mail synchronously (not queued) during an HTTP request with Debugbar enabled and `options.mail.timeline` set to `true` (the default).
2. The `MessageSending` event fires with one `spl_object_id`.
3. The `MessageSent` event fires with a different `spl_object_id` (because `__get('message')` calls `getOriginalMessage()` which returns a different instance).
4. `TimeDataCollector::stopMeasure()` throws `DebugBarException`.

## Workaround

Disable the mail timeline in `config/debugbar.php`:

```php
'options' => [
    'mail' => [
        'timeline' => false,
    ],
],
```

## Environment

- PHP: 8.3
- Laravel: 12.x
- barryvdh/laravel-debugbar: 4.0.8
- php-debugbar/php-debugbar: 3.4.0


## Disclaimer
This ticket was cleaned up via Claude Code during debugging of this issue

title: Error while changing notification subject via listener
author: MichaelAben
body:
The error "Failed stopping measure '$name' because it hasn't been started" from DebugBar\DataCollector\TimeDataCollector:100 is triggered because the subject of a notification that is being sent is changed by a listener on the Illuminate\Mail\Events\MessageSending, which is called later than the Fruitcake\LaravelDebugbar\CollectorProviders\MailCollectorProvider is starting the measure at line 29.

So if your application wants to add the LOCAL tag on an email notification when a project is running locally (my usecase). It needs to be done in an event listener, but if that listener is triggered later than when startMeasure is called, the measure name is going to be changed and thus trow this error when stopping the measure.
